steps.title: Invert the oxygen measure
steps.content: The passage says the manganese-to-calcium ratio inversely correlates with oxygenation. That means a higher Mn/Ca ratio indicates lower oxygenation, while a stable Mn/Ca ratio indicates little change in oxygenation.

steps.title: Match each location
steps.content: Around the Alboran Sea decline, near 9,000 years ago, the graph shows Mn/Ca rising substantially, so oxygenation was falling there. Around the Mauritanian coast decline, near 11,000 years ago, the graph shows little local change in Mn/Ca, so oxygenation was not changing much there.

steps.title: Use the asymmetric evidence
steps.content: That difference explains the researchers' conclusion. A local oxygenation drop coincides with the Alboran Sea population decline, so oxygenation may have mattered there. The same kind of local oxygenation change is absent near the Mauritanian coast, so the graph does not support oxygenation as the cause there.

steps.title: Reject reversed claims
steps.content: Choice A wrongly says oxygenation increased in the Alboran Sea. Choice C correctly starts with higher Alboran oxygenation before the decline but wrongly says Mauritanian oxygenation stayed relatively low; low Mn/Ca implies relatively high oxygenation. Choice D reverses the overall location comparison and does not explain the local decline pattern. Choice B states the asymmetric pattern: declining oxygenation in the Alboran Sea, little oxygenation change near Mauritania. The answer is B.
